summary: Russia launched a large-scale combined attack on Ukraine overnight, deploying an Iskander-M ballistic missile and approximately 90 drones, targeting multiple regions including Kyiv, Kharkiv, Zaporizhzhia, and Sumy, causing civilian casualties and infrastructure damage. The European Commission disbursed the first €3.2 billion tranche of macro-financial assistance to Ukraine, with a separate €6 billion defense package for drone procurement to follow in coming days. On the eastern front, Russian forces continued offensive operations near Kupyansk, Slavyansk, and Konstantinovka, while Ukraine's GRU 'East' units seized strongholds in the Dnepropetrovsk region.
key_points: Russia attacked Ukraine overnight with an Iskander-M ballistic missile and approximately 90 drones, targeting Kyiv, Kharkiv, Zaporizhzhia, Sumy, and other regions, causing civilian casualties and infrastructure damage. (Source: @UkraineNow/82162)

The European Commission disbursed €3.2 billion to Ukraine as the first tranche of a new macro-financial assistance package, with a €6 billion defense allocation for drone procurement to follow shortly. (Source: @DeepStateUA/23594)
Ukrainian President Zelensky approved a 40-day SBU 'influence operation on the Russian Federation' aimed at forcing Moscow to end hostilities. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31171)

Russian forces continued offensive operations on the Kupyansk front with indications of progress in Glushkovka, Kurilovka, and Kurilovka-1, and were advancing south of Rai-Alexandrovka on the Slavyansk front. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31151)
Ukraine's GRU 'East' seized strongholds on the Velikomikhailovka-Alexandrovka line in the Dnepropetrovsk region, with Ukrainian losses reported at 37 servicemen. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31151)
The Russian Ministry of Defense claimed that 660 Ukrainian drones were shot down overnight over Russian regions and the waters of the Black and Azov seas. (Source: @tass_agency/382460)

Ukraine's SBU and the FBI jointly exposed a Russian cyber-espionage network targeting messengers of military personnel, politicians, and activists in Ukraine, Europe, and the US. (Source: @UkraineNow/82167)

The EU announced total support to Ukraine since the invasion began has reached €211.3 billion, with a further €90 billion planned over 2026–2027 under the Ukraine Support Loan framework. (Source: @DeepStateUA/23594)

sections.executive_summary: Russia launched a large-scale overnight attack on Ukraine using an Iskander-M ballistic missile and approximately 90 drones across multiple oblasts, causing civilian casualties and energy infrastructure damage. The European Commission disbursed the first €3.2 billion tranche of a new macro-financial assistance package to Ukraine, alongside a forthcoming €6 billion defense allocation for drone procurement. On the eastern front, Russian forces maintained offensive momentum near Kupyansk, Slavyansk, and Konstantinovka, while Ukraine's GRU 'East' reported tactical gains in the Dnepropetrovsk region.

points: President Zelensky approved a 40-day SBU 'influence operation on the Russian Federation' designed to force Moscow to end hostilities, signaling an expansion of Ukraine's asymmetric warfare strategy beyond the battlefield. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31171)

The SBU jointly with the FBI exposed a Russian cyber-espionage network that systematically targeted messengers of Ukrainian military personnel, politicians, and activists in Ukraine, Europe, and the US using phishing messages disguised as official support bots. (Source: @UkraineNow/82167)

Ukraine received the first €3.2 billion tranche from the EU's new macro-financial assistance under the Ukraine Support Loan framework, with a second tranche of €3.7 billion expected in September and a third of €1.45 billion by year-end. (Source: @DeepStateUA/23594)

A separate EU defense package of €6 billion for drone procurement will be disbursed in coming days, part of a total €28.3 billion earmarked for Ukraine's defense industrial support in 2026. (Source: @DeepStateUA/23594)

Ukrainian assault units on the Kharkiv front continued infantry battles in Losevka, Ukrainka, and Zemlyany Yar on the Volchansk sector, and in Petro-Ivanovka on the Velikoburlyuk sector, while GRU 'North' operated in Kachiy Lopan. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31151)

On the Sumy front, the assault aircraft of the 'North' airborne division drove out the last enemy groups from Ivolzhanskoe and liberated the settlement, with skirmishes continuing in Bachevsk in the Shostka district. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31151)

In the Dnepropetrovsk region, GRU 'East' units seized strongholds on the Velikomikhailovka-Alexandrovka line, and in eastern Zaporizhzhia continued penetrating defenses west of Vozdvizhevka. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31151)

Russia launched an Iskander-M ballistic missile and approximately 90 drones in an overnight attack on multiple Ukrainian regions including Kyiv, Kharkiv, Zaporizhzhia, Sumy, and Poltava, causing civilian casualties and infrastructure damage. (Source: @UkraineNow/82162)

The Russian Ministry of Defense claimed that 660 Ukrainian drones were shot down overnight over Russian regions, the Black Sea, and the Sea of Azov, indicating sustained Ukrainian drone operations deep into Russian territory. (Source: @tass_agency/382460)
On the Kupyansk front, Russian forces advanced east of Oskol with indications of progress in the built-up areas of Glushkovka, Kurilovka, and Kurilovka-1. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31151)

On the Slavyansk front, Russian forces advanced south of the captured Rai-Alexandrovka and on the flanks, with the broad front of intensive offensive actions described as indicating serious intentions by Russian military leadership. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31151)

The heavy assault on Konstantinovka was described as nearing its conclusion, with Russian forces eliminating scattered Ukrainian groups in built-up areas and taking prisoners, with only individual microdistricts on the northwest entrance remaining under Ukrainian control. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31151)

Russian forces carried out strikes on Sumy, Zaporozhye, Dnepropetrovsk, and Poltava regions, including a 'Gerni' strike on an industrial facility in Poltava region and a gas distribution substation hit in Zaporozhye region. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31151)

In Belgorod region, a man received barotrauma from an FPV drone in Novaya Tavolzhanka, and in Shebekino a drone struck a cargo truck resulting in a shrapnel injury, with several districts under attack. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31151)

On the Kupyansk front, Russian forces continued offensive operations east of Oskol with reported progress in the built-up areas of Glushkovka, Kurilovka, and Kurilovka-1. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31151)

On the Slavyansk front, Russian forces advanced south of Rai-Alexandrovka and on the flanks, with intensive offensive actions across a broad front indicating serious operational intent by Russian military leadership. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31151)

The assault on Konstantinovka was nearing its conclusion, with Russian forces eliminating scattered Ukrainian groups in built-up areas and taking prisoners; only individual microdistricts on the northwest entrance remained under Ukrainian or gray zone control. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31151)
In the Dnepropetrovsk region, Ukraine's GRU 'East' seized strongholds on the Velikomikhailovka-Alexandrovka line, with Ukrainian losses reported at 37 servicemen. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31151)
In eastern Zaporizhzhia, GRU 'East' assault groups continued penetrating Ukrainian defenses west of Vozdvizhevka. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31151)

On the Kharkiv front, GRU 'North' continued offensive actions in Kachiy Lopan and adjacent forested areas, while Ukrainian assault units fought in Losevka, Ukrainka, Zemlyany Yar, and Petro-Ivanovka. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31151)
On the Sumy front, the 'North' airborne division's assault aircraft drove out the last enemy groups from Ivolzhanskoe and liberated the settlement. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31151)
Skirmishes continued in and around Bachevsk in the Shostka district on the Sumy front. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31151)

In Belgorod region, a man received acute barotrauma from an FPV drone detonation in Novaya Tavolzhanka, and in Shebekino a drone hit a cargo truck with a man hospitalized for shrapnel wounds. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31151)
Several districts in Belgorod region were reported under attack, with Russian forces conducting strikes on Sumy region. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31151)
Ukrainian drones were reported heading toward Kharkiv from the south and southeast, and toward Sumy from the north, indicating continued cross-border drone operations. (Source: @kpszsu/66430)

In the Zaporizhzhia region, Russian forces hit a gas distribution substation, and restoration work to restore energy supply was ongoing alongside battles against enemy drones disrupting logistics. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31151)
Ukraine's GRU 'East' continued penetrating enemy defenses west of Vozdvizhevka in eastern Zaporizhzhia. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31151)

In the Kherson region, one civilian was killed and six injured over the previous 24 hours, with UAV attacks on the Chaplynka-Nadezhivka highway and in populated areas including Razdolnoye, Raden, and Veliki Kopan', with damage recorded in 14 populated areas. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31151)

Regarding the Kinburn Spit, DeepStateUA confirmed there was no confirmed active Russian withdrawal, and the enemy continued using the area for 'Molniya' drone launches toward Mykolaiv and Kherson, with electronic warfare assets also present. (Source: @DeepStateUA/23595)

A Ukrainian flag was dropped from a drone onto the Kinburn Spit, described as a positive but non-operational event, and the Ukrainian Navy spokesperson confirmed the area remains a zone of combat operations. (Source: @DeepStateUA/23595)

Russia deployed ballistic missiles against Poltava (2 missiles targeting Kremenchuk), Kyiv (2 missiles), and other cities, with air raid alerts reported across multiple oblasts including Zhytomyr, Rivne, Chernihiv, and Sumy. (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68040, @mon1tor_ua/68064, @mon1tor_ua/68061)
Approximately 90 drones were deployed by Russia in the overnight attack, with targets including Kyiv, Kharkiv, Zaporizhzhia, Sumy, and Poltava regions. (Source: @UkraineNow/82162)

The Russian Ministry of Defense claimed 660 Ukrainian drones were shot down overnight over Russian territory and adjacent waters, suggesting a significant scale of Ukrainian drone operations. (Source: @tass_agency/382460)

Ukrainian drone groups were reported heading toward Kharkiv from the south and southeast, toward Sumy from the north, toward Zaporizhzhia from eastern Dnipropetrovsk, and along the Chernihiv-Belarus border moving westward. (Source: @kpszsu/66430, @kpszsu/66434, @kpszsu/66395, @kpszsu/66438)
Several drones targeting the capital were shot down during the night, with combat against enemy drones also reported in Crimea and Sevastopol. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31151)

In Krasnodar Region, a fire broke out at an oil depot in Poltava stanitsa of Krasnoarmeysky district as a result of a drone attack, temporarily blocking traffic on the highway to Trudobelikovsky farm. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31151)
The relatively infrequent Ukrainian drone and missile attacks in recent days may indicate a serious buildup of strike weapons, according to the Two Majors report. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31151)

key_developments: Russia launched a combined overnight attack with an Iskander-M ballistic missile and approximately 90 drones targeting Kyiv, Kharkiv, Zaporizhzhia, Sumy, and Poltava, causing civilian casualties including four injured in Sumy Oblast and five in Bogodukhiv. (Source: @UkraineNow/82162)

The European Commission disbursed €3.2 billion to Ukraine as the first MFA tranche, with a €6 billion defense package for drone procurement to follow within days, as part of a total €90 billion Ukraine Support Loan over 2026–2027. (Source: @DeepStateUA/23594)

President Zelensky approved a 40-day SBU influence operation against the Russian Federation aimed at forcing Moscow to end hostilities, signaling an escalation in Ukraine's strategic approach. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31171)

Russian forces advanced on the Kupyansk front with progress in Glushkovka, Kurilovka, and Kurilovka-1, and on the Slavyansk front south of Rai-Alexandrovka, while the assault on Konstantinovka neared its conclusion. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31151)

Ukraine's GRU 'East' seized strongholds on the Velikomikhailovka-Alexandrovka line in Dnipropetrovsk region with Ukrainian losses of 37 servicemen, and continued penetrating defenses west of Vozdvizhevka in Zaporizhzhia. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31151)

The Russian Ministry of Defense reported 660 Ukrainian drones shot down overnight, indicating a substantial Ukrainian drone campaign targeting Russian territory and infrastructure. (Source: @tass_agency/382460)

The SBU and FBI jointly exposed a Russian cyber-espionage network targeting military personnel, politicians, and activists across Ukraine, Europe, and the US through phishing attacks disguised as official support bots. (Source: @UkraineNow/82167)
sections.threat_assessment: The operational picture shows a significant escalation in Russian long-range strike activity, combining ballistic missiles and massed drone attacks across multiple Ukrainian oblasts simultaneously, with energy infrastructure in Poltava and Zaporizhzhia as primary targets. The Russian MoD's claim of 660 Ukrainian drones intercepted in a single night suggests Ukraine is conducting an increasingly intensive deep-strike campaign against Russian territory, likely targeting logistics and energy assets. On the ground, Russian forces are maintaining offensive pressure across multiple sectors — Kupyansk, Slavyansk, Konstantinovka, and Kharkiv — with the Konstantinovka assault approaching culmination. The approval of a 40-day SBU influence operation against Russia signals Ukraine's intent to expand asymmetric operations, potentially including sabotage, information operations, or targeted actions inside Russian territory.
sections.regional_response: The European Commission's disbursement of €3.2 billion in macro-financial assistance, with an additional €6 billion defense package for drones imminent, demonstrates the EU's continued commitment to Ukraine's fiscal stability and military capacity under the Ukraine Support Loan framework totaling €90 billion over 2026–2027. The US State Department endorsed Zelensky's peace proposals, though Russian rejection places President Trump in a difficult diplomatic position. Ukraine fulfilled seven political conditions to receive the first MFA tranche, including extending military conscription, submitting legislation on digital platform taxation and VAT reform, and advancing customs alignment with EU acquis. The total EU support since the invasion reached €211.3 billion, with €3.8 billion derived from frozen Russian asset revenues.
sections.intelligence_notes: The Two Majors report noted that the relative paucity of Ukrainian drone and missile launches in recent days may indicate a significant buildup of strike weapons, suggesting a potential forthcoming escalation in Ukraine's deep-strike campaign. The FSB's claim that Ukrainian special services coordinated a Dagestanian teenager's terrorist network for school attacks represents a Russian intelligence narrative that warrants monitoring but remains unverified. The reported inability to implement Syrsky's 60-day mandatory rotation order, according to First Deputy Military Ombudsman Ruslan Tsygankov, suggests ongoing personnel management challenges within frontline units. The Belgorod region injuries from FPV drones and cargo truck strikes indicate that Russian border regions remain vulnerable to Ukrainian drone operations despite claimed air defense successes.
